highlight.title: MCP Registry Goes GA with 2,000+ Servers and Security Scrutiny
highlight.summary: <cite index="27-10,27-11">The MCP Registry launched in preview with close to two thousand entries since September, a 407% growth from the initial batch</cite>. <cite index="25-18,25-19">Unofficial registries now index over 16,000 MCP servers, but security research reveals 88% require credentials with 53% relying on insecure static secrets</cite>.

resources.title: State of MCP Server Security 2025: Research Report
resources.why_now: <cite index="25-27,25-28">Security analysis of 5,200+ MCP servers reveals 88% require credentials but over half use insecure static secrets, with only 8.5% using OAuth</cite>. Critical for enterprise adoption planning.

resources.title: From standard to ecosystem: the new MCP updates
resources.why_now: <cite index="30-2,30-9">November 25 release introduces async operations, stateless architecture, and registry GA, moving MCP from standard to interoperable ecosystem for production AI systems</cite>.

resources.title: Claude Code's Two Major Updates Take the Market by Storm
resources.why_now: <cite index="38-7,38-8">MCP tool search solves context bloat by dynamic loading instead of preloading all tool descriptions, which was consuming 20k-60k tokens at startup</cite>. Game-changer for scalability.

resources.title: AI vs Gen Z: How AI has changed the career pathway for junior developers
resources.why_now: <cite index="19-11,19-13">Software developer employment for ages 22-25 declined 20% from late 2022 peak, while AI tool usage reached 84% of developers by 2025</cite>. Fundamental shift in entry-level roles.

claude_take: <cite index="18-11,18-12">MCP will usher in autonomous agents that can manage multiple development steps with little human intervention, like Cognition's Devin AI software engineer</cite>. <cite index="30-18,30-19,30-20">The protocol is evolving from specification to infrastructure, reducing integration barriers for developers while enabling scalable, governed interoperability for organizations</cite>.
